21.title: Build Slack integration for real-time alert notifications
21.description: We want to send alert notifications to customer Slack channels. This requires building an OAuth flow for Slack, a channel selector UI, message formatting templates, and a delivery pipeline. Should support both public and private channels.
21.summary: Add a new Slack integration so customers can receive platform alerts in their own Slack workspaces, including OAuth, channel selection, message templates, and delivery.

20.title: Webhook retry logic ignores exponential backoff
20.description: The webhook delivery system retries failed webhooks every 10 seconds regardless of failure count. It should use exponential backoff: 10s, 30s, 90s, 270s. The retry logic is in webhook_dispatcher.py line 45. Currently it hardcodes the interval.
20.summary: Failed webhooks are retried on a fixed 10-second interval instead of the intended exponential backoff schedule (10s/30s/90s/270s). The hardcoded interval in webhook_dispatcher.py needs to be replaced with backoff based on attempt count.

19.title: Update user-facing error messages to new copy guidelines
19.description: Product and content teams published new error message guidelines (see Confluence doc). We need to update all user-facing error strings to match the new tone and format. There are approximately 80+ error messages across 12 modules.
19.summary: Rewrite roughly 80+ user-facing error strings across 12 modules to conform to the new tone and formatting guidelines published by Product and Content.

18.title: Investigate slow response times on /api/reports endpoint
18.description: The /api/reports endpoint is averaging 4.2s response time, up from 800ms last quarter. Needs investigation. Could be a missing index, an N+1 query, or increased data volume. No clear root cause yet.
18.summary: The /api/reports endpoint's average latency has regressed from ~800ms to ~4.2s over the last quarter. Root cause is unknown and needs diagnosis (possible missing index, N+1 query, or data volume growth).

17.title: Fix broken pagination on audit log page
17.description: The audit log page shows only the first 25 entries and the 'Next' button does nothing. Console shows a JS error: 'Cannot read property offset of undefined'. The pagination component works on other pages, so this is likely a prop-passing issue specific to the audit log view.
17.summary: The audit log page is stuck on the first 25 entries because the Next button throws a JS error ('Cannot read property offset of undefined'). The shared pagination component works elsewhere, so the audit log view is likely passing props incorrectly.

16.title: Migrate notification service to event-driven architecture
16.description: Our current notification service uses synchronous HTTP calls to send emails and push notifications. We should migrate to an event-driven model using a message queue. This will improve reliability and decouple services. Requires evaluating RabbitMQ vs Kafka and updating 4-5 downstream consumers.
16.summary: Replace the notification service's synchronous HTTP calls with an event-driven model backed by a message queue (RabbitMQ or Kafka), updating 4-5 downstream consumers to improve reliability and decoupling.

15.title: Tooltip text truncated on billing summary page
15.description: The tooltip on the 'Total charges' row in the billing summary gets cut off when the text is longer than 40 characters. Likely a CSS max-width issue on the tooltip container.
15.summary: The 'Total charges' tooltip on the billing summary page is cut off for text longer than 40 characters, likely due to a CSS max-width constraint on the tooltip container.

14.title: Add dark mode support across the platform
14.description: Multiple customers have requested dark mode. This would require updating the design system tokens, all component themes, and adding a user preference toggle. We should also consider OS-level preference detection.
14.summary: Introduce a platform-wide dark mode, including design system token updates, theming for all components, a user preference toggle, and OS-level preference detection.

13.title: Dashboard CSV export includes soft-deleted records
13.description: When exporting dashboard data to CSV, rows that were soft-deleted still appear in the export. The query in export_service.py does not filter on the is_deleted flag. Expected behavior: only active records should be included.
13.summary: Dashboard CSV exports incorrectly include soft-deleted rows because the query in export_service.py does not filter on the is_deleted flag. Only active records should be exported.

12.title: Login returns 500 when email contains a plus sign
12.description: Users with emails like jane+work@example.com get a 500 Internal Server Error on the login page. The server logs show an unescaped character in the email validation regex. This affects roughly 2% of signups based on our analytics.
12.summary: Logging in with a sub-addressed email (e.g. jane+work@example.com) crashes with a 500 error due to an unescaped character in the email validation regex, blocking roughly 2% of signups.

12.title: Login returns 500 when email contains a plus sign
12.description: Users with emails like jane+work@example.com get a 500 Internal Server Error on the login page. The server logs show an unescaped character in the email validation regex. This affects roughly 2% of signups based on our analytics.
12.summary: Logging in with a sub-addressed email (e.g. jane+work@example.com) crashes with a 500 error due to an unescaped character in the email validation regex, blocking roughly 2% of signups.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: High-impact login failure blocking ~2% of signups with a clear regex fix, but flagged risk=high means autonomous resolution is gated on human review before shipping to the auth path.

12.scope_summary: Touches the email validation regex in the auth service's login path; auth-critical so any change needs careful review.

20.title: Webhook retry logic ignores exponential backoff
20.description: The webhook delivery system retries failed webhooks every 10 seconds regardless of failure count. It should use exponential backoff: 10s, 30s, 90s, 270s. The retry logic is in webhook_dispatcher.py line 45. Currently it hardcodes the interval.
20.summary: Failed webhooks are retried on a fixed 10-second interval instead of the intended exponential backoff schedule (10s/30s/90s/270s). The hardcoded interval in webhook_dispatcher.py needs to be replaced with backoff based on attempt count.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Clear, narrow bug with a specific file and line identified and a well-defined backoff schedule; fixing it restores intended webhook reliability for all integration customers.

implementation_options: Replace the hardcoded 10s interval in webhook_dispatcher.py with a computed delay based on attempt count (10s, 30s, 90s, 270s)
Introduce a small helper that returns the backoff delay for a given attempt, then call it from the retry scheduler
Store attempt count on the webhook job record and compute next_retry_at using exponential backoff when a delivery fails
20.scope_summary: Touches the webhook retry scheduler in webhook_dispatcher.py; no schema or downstream consumer changes required.

13.title: Dashboard CSV export includes soft-deleted records
13.description: When exporting dashboard data to CSV, rows that were soft-deleted still appear in the export. The query in export_service.py does not filter on the is_deleted flag. Expected behavior: only active records should be included.
13.summary: Dashboard CSV exports incorrectly include soft-deleted rows because the query in export_service.py does not filter on the is_deleted flag. Only active records should be exported.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Data-correctness bug with an obvious fix (filter on is_deleted in export_service.py), but risk=high because exported CSVs feed customer reporting and potentially compliance workflows, so it needs human sign-off.

13.scope_summary: Touches the export query in export_service.py; affects CSV outputs consumed by customers and potentially downstream reporting.

17.title: Fix broken pagination on audit log page
17.description: The audit log page shows only the first 25 entries and the 'Next' button does nothing. Console shows a JS error: 'Cannot read property offset of undefined'. The pagination component works on other pages, so this is likely a prop-passing issue specific to the audit log view.
17.summary: The audit log page is stuck on the first 25 entries because the Next button throws a JS error ('Cannot read property offset of undefined'). The shared pagination component works elsewhere, so the audit log view is likely passing props incorrectly.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Concrete JS error with a strong hypothesis (prop-passing mismatch versus the shared pagination component) and a narrow, isolated fix on the audit log view; audit visibility matters for compliance users.

implementation_options: Diff the audit log page's usage of the pagination component against a working page and align the props (offset, limit, total)
Add a defensive default for the offset prop in the pagination component so undefined inputs don't throw
Wire the audit log list's pagination state (page, pageSize, total) through to the shared component using the same contract as other pages
17.scope_summary: Touches the audit log page's integration with the shared pagination component; no backend or schema changes expected.

18.title: Investigate slow response times on /api/reports endpoint
18.description: The /api/reports endpoint is averaging 4.2s response time, up from 800ms last quarter. Needs investigation. Could be a missing index, an N+1 query, or increased data volume. No clear root cause yet.
18.summary: The /api/reports endpoint's average latency has regressed from ~800ms to ~4.2s over the last quarter. Root cause is unknown and needs diagnosis (possible missing index, N+1 query, or data volume growth).

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Significant latency regression on a customer-facing endpoint, but it is an open-ended investigation with no identified root cause, which is poorly suited to autonomous resolution.

18.scope_summary: Potentially touches the /api/reports query path, database indexes, and ORM usage; blast radius unknown until diagnosed.

15.title: Tooltip text truncated on billing summary page
15.description: The tooltip on the 'Total charges' row in the billing summary gets cut off when the text is longer than 40 characters. Likely a CSS max-width issue on the tooltip container.
15.summary: The 'Total charges' tooltip on the billing summary page is cut off for text longer than 40 characters, likely due to a CSS max-width constraint on the tooltip container.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Likely a trivial CSS width fix, but risk=high (billing surface) means it should not ship autonomously without a human confirming nothing else in the billing summary layout regresses.

15.scope_summary: Touches the tooltip styling on the billing summary page; scoped to CSS but on a revenue-facing view.

19.title: Update user-facing error messages to new copy guidelines
19.description: Product and content teams published new error message guidelines (see Confluence doc). We need to update all user-facing error strings to match the new tone and format. There are approximately 80+ error messages across 12 modules.
19.summary: Rewrite roughly 80+ user-facing error strings across 12 modules to conform to the new tone and formatting guidelines published by Product and Content.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Low user and business impact copy work spread across ~80 strings in 12 modules, and the new tone/format guidelines require editorial judgment that is hard to apply autonomously at quality.

19.scope_summary: Touches roughly 80+ user-facing error strings across 12 modules; no behavior changes, but broad surface area.

21.title: Build Slack integration for real-time alert notifications
21.description: We want to send alert notifications to customer Slack channels. This requires building an OAuth flow for Slack, a channel selector UI, message formatting templates, and a delivery pipeline. Should support both public and private channels.
21.summary: Add a new Slack integration so customers can receive platform alerts in their own Slack workspaces, including OAuth, channel selection, message templates, and delivery.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Net-new integration requiring OAuth flow, UI, templating, and delivery pipeline; too broad and product-design-heavy to resolve autonomously with confidence.

21.scope_summary: Touches a new Slack OAuth/integration service, a channel selector UI, message template system, and the alert delivery pipeline.

14.title: Add dark mode support across the platform
14.description: Multiple customers have requested dark mode. This would require updating the design system tokens, all component themes, and adding a user preference toggle. We should also consider OS-level preference detection.
14.summary: Introduce a platform-wide dark mode, including design system token updates, theming for all components, a user preference toggle, and OS-level preference detection.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Large design-system effort spanning tokens, every component theme, and a preference toggle; requires product and design decisions not suitable for autonomous execution.

14.scope_summary: Touches design system tokens, all themed components, user preference storage, and OS-level preference detection.

16.title: Migrate notification service to event-driven architecture
16.description: Our current notification service uses synchronous HTTP calls to send emails and push notifications. We should migrate to an event-driven model using a message queue. This will improve reliability and decouple services. Requires evaluating RabbitMQ vs Kafka and updating 4-5 downstream consumers.
16.summary: Replace the notification service's synchronous HTTP calls with an event-driven model backed by a message queue (RabbitMQ or Kafka), updating 4-5 downstream consumers to improve reliability and decoupling.

planner_score.recommendation_reason: Architectural migration requiring a broker choice (RabbitMQ vs Kafka) and updates to 4-5 consumers; too ambiguous and high-blast-radius for autonomous resolution.

16.scope_summary: Touches the notification service, a new message queue, and 4-5 downstream consumer services.
